patterns.name: Per-module test mirror
patterns.description: tests/<module>/ 1:1 mirrors src/<module>/ with colocated _data_/ fixtures. Removes the 'where does this test live?' decision.

patterns.name: Optional feature flag for secondary binary
patterns.description: Cargo.toml marks clap + directories deps as optional=true; Makefile build-gui target activates --features='gui'. Keeps default binary lean.

patterns.name: Strict dependency pinning (=X.Y.Z)
patterns.description: Every direct dep uses = prefix, refusing caret/tilde auto-upgrade. Reproducibility over convenience. Candidate for SoNash meta-pipeline scripts (scripts/reviews, scripts/skills).

patterns.name: Multi-channel distribution catalog
patterns.description: 18 install channels documented inline in README, each as a one-line reproducible install. Treats packaging as a product dimension.

knowledge.name: Single-file archival as a content type
knowledge.description: Data-URL inlining of CSS/images/JS into one HTML5 document. Reference implementation for 'capture this article inside the journal' feature, when SoNash external-references storage decision surfaces.

knowledge.name: CLI + managed-service wrapper (Apify Actor)
knowledge.description: Same CLI wrapped as Apify Actor SaaS. Validates the 'deterministic worker + authenticated callable wrapper' pattern SoNash already uses (httpsCallable in front of Firestore logic).

knowledge.name: CC0-1.0 public-domain licensing for tooling
knowledge.description: Zero-friction extract/fork permission. Informational for any future SoNash extract decision from this repo.

anti_patterns.name: Pinning philosophy contradicted by Dockerfile
anti_patterns.description: Cargo.toml pins every dep with =X.Y.Z, but Dockerfile fetches the latest release via unpinned curl to api.github.com/releases/latest. Reproducibility claim broken at the container layer. Lesson: supply-chain discipline must be uniform across artifacts, not per-file.

anti_patterns.name: Mono-README without SECURITY.md
anti_patterns.description: Tool that fetches arbitrary web content ships zero vulnerability-disclosure channel. 15k stars × arbitrary URL input = attack surface; no SECURITY.md means no coordinated disclosure path. Lesson: SECURITY.md earns its cost at ~1k stars, not at 100k.
